questions.question: Explain the difference between function templates and class templates in C++. Provide one example of each.
keyPoints: Function templates define a family of functions; class templates define a family of classes
Function templates are automatically instantiated when called; class templates need explicit instantiation
Example of function template: template<typename T> T maximum(T a, T b) { return (a > b) ? a : b; }
Example of class template: template<typename T> class Stack { T elements[100]; };

questions.question: What are non-type template parameters in C++? Give an example of a class template that uses a non-type parameter.
keyPoints: Non-type template parameters accept compile-time constant values
They can be integers, pointers, or references
Not the same as type parameters (typename/class)
Example: template<int size> class Array { T arr[size]; };

questions.question: Define template specialization and explain its purpose with an example.
keyPoints: Template specialization provides custom implementation for specific data types
Full specialization: specialize all template parameters
Purpose: Optimize for specific types or handle types that don't work with generic code
Example: template<> class Stack<char> { /* specialized for char */ };

questions.question: What is the role of the 'typename' keyword in C++ templates? When is it mandatory to use 'typename'?
keyPoints: 'typename' declares type parameters in templates
It can be used interchangeably with 'class'
Mandatory when accessing nested type names dependent on template parameter
Example: typename T::iterator iter; - iterator is a nested type of T

questions.question: Write a template function to swap two values of any data type. Also explain how the compiler handles this template.
keyPoints: Function template syntax with type parameter T
Uses pass-by-reference to modify original values
Compiler generates specific function for each type used
Example: template<typename T> void swap(T& a, T& b) { T temp = a; a = b; b = temp; }

questions.question: Explain the concept of default template arguments in C++ class templates with an example.
keyPoints: Default template arguments provide fallback types when none specified
Can be used with both class and function templates
Syntax: template<typename T = int> class Stack { };
Allows: Stack<> s; // uses int by default

questions.question: What is SFINAE in C++? Explain its significance in template programming.
keyPoints: SFINAE stands for Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
Compiler silently moves to next overload when template substitution fails
Enables compile-time polymorphism and type traits
Allows writing code that adapts to available operations on types

questions.question: Design a template class 'Pair' that can store two values of potentially different types. Include: (a) Constructor (b) Getter functions for both values (c) A function template to compare two pairs. Write complete C++ code.
keyPoints: Class template with two type parameters: template<typename T1, typename T2>
Private members: T1 first, T2 second
Constructor: Pair(T1 a, T2 b) : first(a), second(b) {}
Getters: T1 getFirst() const { return first; }
Comparison function: template<typename U, typename V> bool compare(const Pair<U,V>& p1, const Pair<U,V>& p2)

questions.question: (a) What is the difference between full specialization and partial specialization of class templates? (b) Write a C++ program demonstrating partial template specialization for a pointer wrapper class.
keyPoints: Full specialization: template<> class MyClass<int> { } - specialized for all parameters
Partial specialization: template<typename T> class MyClass<T*> { } - some parameters specialized
Partial works only with class templates, not function templates
Demonstrate: generic template for all types, specialized template for pointer types

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program to implement a generic Stack class template with push, pop, and display operations. Handle edge cases and include appropriate error messages. Also show how to instantiate this stack for integers and characters.
keyPoints: Class template: template<typename T> class Stack
Private: vector<T> or array with top index
Methods: push(T), pop(), isEmpty(), display()
Check for stack overflow/underflow conditions
Instantiation: Stack<int> intStack; Stack<char> charStack;

questions.question: Explain the concept of member function templates and template function overloading in C++. Provide examples demonstrating when the compiler chooses each version.
keyPoints: Member template: template function inside a non-template or template class
Template overloading: multiple template functions with different parameters
Function overloading: template vs regular function
Compiler selection: most specific match is chosen
Example: swap(int&, int&) vs template<typename T> swap(T&, T&)
